title: Cannot run MongoDB in systemd-container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a systemd container based on centos/systemd

2. Install rh-mongodb34-mongod inside container

3. Start container with `podman ... --systemd=true ...`

**Describe the results you received:**

The service cannot start inside the container, it fails with the following error:

> New main PID 817 does not belong to service, and PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Prior to CentOS 7.7 and https://access.redhat.com/solutions/4420581 everything worked great.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.4.4
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.3
  podman version: 1.4.4
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.4.4-4.el7.centos.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 0.3.0, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"centos"'
    version: "7"
  MemFree: 9602150400
  MemTotal: 16656789504
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-65.rc8.el7.centos.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: foreman-chbr-node1.vms.dev.basalt.se
  kernel: 3.10.0-957.12.2.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 1h 5m 18.04s (Approximately 0.04 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.4.4-4.el7.centos.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Virtual machine running on OpenStack, having run `yum -y update` on the host prior to starting the container.

Same version of systemd on host and container.

I have also filed an issue with Foreman in case their installation and configuration of MongoDB is the issue: https://projects.theforeman.org/issues/27887

But I am starting to suspect that the issue is with running a systemd container with `systemd-219-67` and I suspected podman to fix all the mounts when using the `--systemd` flag.

comments.body:
I have an easy way to reproduce the issue:

```
$ cat Dockerfile
FROM centos/systemd
RUN yum -y update && \
    yum -y install centos-release-scl && \
    yum -y install rh-mongodb34-mongodb-server && \
    systemctl enable rh-mongodb34-mongod.service

$ sudo podman build -t test .

... snip ...

$ sudo podman run --systemd=true --name test -d --privileged test
19ae5a643b6b71223068c329b167f9135f3603e32e0e8d5fb920df562c389034

$ sudo podman exec -it test systemctl status rh-mongodb34-mongod
● rh-mongodb34-mongod.service - High-performance, schema-free document-oriented database
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/rh-mongodb34-mongod.service; enabled; vendor preset: disabled)
   Active: activating (start) since Fri 2019-10-04 11:03:03 UTC; 1min 28s ago
  Process: 75 ExecStart=/opt/rh/rh-mongodb34/root/usr/libexec/mongodb-scl-helper enable $RH_MONGODB34_SCLS_ENABLED -- /opt/rh/rh-mongodb34/root/usr/bin/mongod $OPTIONS run (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
   CGroup: /machine.slice/libpod-19ae5a643b6b71223068c329b167f9135f3603e32e0e8d5fb920df562c389034.scope/system.slice/rh-mongodb34-mongod.service
           └─193 /opt/rh/rh-mongodb34/root/usr/bin/mongod -f /etc/opt/rh/rh-mongodb34/mongod.conf run

Oct 04 11:03:03 19ae5a643b6b systemd[1]: Starting High-performance, schema-free document-oriented database...
Oct 04 11:03:03 19ae5a643b6b mongodb-scl-helper[75]: about to fork child process, waiting until server is ready for connections.
Oct 04 11:03:03 19ae5a643b6b mongodb-scl-helper[75]: forked process: 193
Oct 04 11:03:04 19ae5a643b6b mongodb-scl-helper[75]: child process started successfully, parent exiting
Oct 04 11:03:04 19ae5a643b6b systemd[1]: New main PID 193 does not belong to service, and PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.
Oct 04 11:03:04 19ae5a643b6b systemd[1]: New main PID 193 does not belong to service, and PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.
```
Seems like a systemd issue.  Could you open a bugzilla on this.  I don't believe this is an upstream issue.
Which bugzilla? Should I open a support ticket in the Customer Portal?

I found a Red Hat Bugzilla and created an account to file this bug report: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1758509

I also opened a support ticket in Customer Portal since it happens on our RHEL servers too.

The interesting part is that it works fine when run directly on the host. It's only inside a container that the issue appears. That's why I suspected that there is something wrong with how the container is run, either a fault by me or by podman.
Thanks this very well could be a bug in podman or in mongodb. But bugzilla is the place to put older versions of the tools, rather then upstream.

Ok, I put the component as systemd but maybe that should be changed to something else then. Didn't see podman or "container tools" there, though.

If it would help I could try the upstream libpod, either now (just to check if it's present in master) or when you have branch with a suggested fix.

With `podman-1.5.1-3.fc30.x86_64` it runs fine:
```
● rh-mongodb34-mongod.service - High-performance, schema-free document-oriented database
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/rh-mongodb34-mongod.service; enabled; vendor preset: disabled)
   Active: active (running) since Sun 2019-10-06 14:17:31 UTC; 49s ago
  Process: 55 ExecStart=/opt/rh/rh-mongodb34/root/usr/libexec/mongodb-scl-helper enable $RH_MONGODB34_SCLS_ENABLED -- /opt/rh/rh-mongodb34/root/usr/bin/mongod $OPTIONS run (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
 Main PID: 69 (mongod)
   CGroup: /machine.slice/libpod-049c34384b9dce05a186656fea08b67e827fdefd504b2e29a7b7404e13727c8f.scope/system.slice/rh-mongodb34-mongod.service
           └─69 /opt/rh/rh-mongodb34/root/usr/bin/mongod -f /etc/opt/rh/rh-mo...

Oct 06 14:17:30 049c34384b9d systemd[1]: Starting High-performance, schema-f....
Oct 06 14:17:30 049c34384b9d mongodb-scl-helper[55]: about to fork child proc...
Oct 06 14:17:30 049c34384b9d mongodb-scl-helper[55]: forked process: 69
Oct 06 14:17:31 049c34384b9d systemd[1]: Started High-performance, schema-fr....
Hint: Some lines were ellipsized, use -l to show in full.

```

Cool! Will the fix be backported to the 1.4 branch anytime soon, since that's what's available on RHEL/CentOS right now?

Also, are there any binaries for CentOS for 1.5 or even 1.6? Or do I need to compile it manually in order to verify that it solves my issue?
@lsm5 Could you point at the Centos 1.5 or 1.6 packages?
Any update on when we can expect to see 1.5 or 1.6 in CentOS (or a 1.4.5 with the fix backported)?
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
I know the packages for podman 1.6 will show up in RHEL 8.1.1 which is scheduled to come out in January.
